section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Introduction of national minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
content: Legislative introduction of mandatory minimum energy classes for the least performing segment of the non-residential building stock (class E by 2030; class D by 2033), including powers to update thresholds
amending_legislation: Energy Efficiency Act (EEOS) – new/amended provisions for CEFs and deadlines

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Legal setting of limit values and methodology for CEFs
content: Legal setting of primary energy consumption limits per type of non-residential building, according to existing regulations
amending_legislation: Regulation No RD-02-20-3 of 9.11.2022 – addition with an explicit link to CEFR

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Introduction of a simplified energy labelling system
content: Establish a simplified procedure to pre-define the energy class of non-residential buildings based on a limited set of input data in order to identify buildings below CEFR
amending_legislation: EEOS – legal basis for an energy label; Regulation No E-RD-04-2 of 16.12.2022 – new section on simplified labelling
sources_of_funding: Life and Competitiveness and Innovation in Enterprises projects

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Digitalisation of registers and data integration
content: Extension of the national energy performance register with energy labels; automated data exchange with cadastre and municipal registers
amending_legislation: Regulation No E-RD-04-1 of 3.1.2018 – supplementing the registers under the Energy Efficiency Act

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Mandatory labelling/certification of non-residential buildings
content: Making it mandatory for all non-residential buildings to have a valid energy certificate or a registered energy label
amending_legislation: EEOS – extension of scope and time limit obligations
sources_of_funding: Private funds; public support for labelling

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Linking CEFs to major renovation and public funding
content: Requirement for demonstrated compliance with CEFs for major renovations and as a condition for access to public funding
amending_legislation: EEOSS; OSH – references in the case of overhaul; Regulation No E-RD-04-2/2022

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Controls instructions and penalties for non-compliance
content: Introduction of a phased mechanism: prescription → restriction of access to public programmes → financial penalty
amending_legislation: EEOS – penal and administrative provisions for CEFs; Regulation to the Energy Efficiency Act

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Integration of CEFs with financial instruments
content: Linking CEFH commitments to targeted financial products and combined funding
amending_legislation: EEOSS; rules of the National Decarbonisation Fund and other funding programmes
sources_of_funding: In the framework of the activities

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Technical assistance and renovation roadmaps
content: Provision of labelling surveys and roadmaps through CCA as part of CEFR implementation
amending_legislation: EEOSS; Regulation No E-RD-04-2/2022 – roadmaps as a result of an audit

section_topic: National minimum energy performance standards (MEPS)
measure_name: Monitoring and annual reporting on compliance
content: Annual report on coverage, compliance and achieved energy and emission effects
amending_legislation: EEOS – reporting obligation; Regulation No E-RD-04-1/2018

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: National building renovation passport scheme
content: Introduction of a building renovation passport as an official document with regulatory content, application cases (one-off/staged deep renovation) and link to the certification system
amending_legislation: Energy Efficiency Act (EEOS) – explicit legal basis and delegation; Regulation to the Energy Efficiency Act on content and procedure

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: Integration of passports with energy performance certificates
content: Rules to avoid duplication: in the case of a valid certificate, the passport shall use its details; in the case of a passport without a valid certificate – data is integrated into the national database/registry
amending_legislation: Regulation No E-RD-04-2 of 16.12.2022 – supplement with a chapter/section on passports

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: National definitions and thresholds for deep and staged deep renovation
content: Regulatory definition of the depth threshold (reaching NZEB/NZEB level or at least 60% primary energy reduction) and conditions for staged renovation (sequential steps within 10-15 years compatible with the final target)
amending_legislation: Regulation No RD-02-20-3 of 9.11.2022 – supplement with definitions/thresholds and link to the passport

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: Digital passport registration and unique identifier
content: Extension of the national register of energy performance certificates to building renovation passports (unique identifier, access rules, traceability and update)
amending_legislation: Regulation No E-RD-04-1 of 3.1.2018 – supplement with passports as a new type of entry

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: Expert requirements and quality control for passports
content: Introduction/fine-tuning of competency requirements, changes to the certification programme, possibilities for continuous training, checks and sanctions for systematic poor quality documents
amending_legislation: Regulation No E-RD-04-2 of 16.12.2022 – supplementing the rules on qualification and quality control

section_topic: Deep and staged deep building renovation
measure_name: Compatibility between the building renovation passport and the technical building passport
content: Regulation of compatibility/references between the building renovation passport and the technical passport (to use available data and avoid duplication in staged renovation)
amending_legislation: Regulation No 5 of 28.12.2006 on the technical passports of construction works – supplement with references/fields for energy data

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: National Framework for the Decarbonisation of Heating and Cooling
content: Put in place a comprehensive national policy for a phased transition to low carbon and climate neutral heating and cooling systems, encompassing individual and centralised solutions
amending_legislation: Energy Act; Energy Efficiency Act; regulations

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Phase-out plan for fossil fuel boilers
content: Ban new investments in fossil fuel boilers, end subsidies and fully replace with RES and low-carbon technologies by 2040
amending_legislation: Regulations to the Energy Act and the Energy Efficiency Act

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Transformation of centralised heating and cooling systems
content: Turning district heating into efficient, low-temperature and flexible networks with an increasing share of RES and waste heat to 100% in 2050

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Local heating and cooling plans
content: Development of mandatory local plans by municipalities above 45,000 inhabitants for RES, waste heat, low-temperature grids and electrification

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Regulatory changes for building systems
content: Requiring common internal installations in new multi-apartment buildings and regulating geothermal solutions
amending_legislation: Spatial Planning Act; EEOSS; Regulation No RD-02-20-3 of 9.11.2022

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Mandatory minimum share of RES when replacing heating
content: Introduction of a minimum 50% RES requirement for the replacement of a heating source in buildings
sources_of_funding: EU funds, national programmes; Private funds

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Modernisation and planning in district heating companies
content: Mandatory five-year plans for decarbonisation, loss reduction, digitalisation and RES integration

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Financial and social support to households
content: Grants, low-interest loans and targeted support for energy poor households to switch to RES heating
sources_of_funding: EU funds, state budget, private funding

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Demonstration and pilot projects heating and cooling energy communities
content: Replicable pilot models (e.g. micronetworks for heating and cooling)
sources_of_funding: EU Funds, EEA Grants; public-private partnerships

section_topic: Decarbonisation of heating and cooling
measure_name: Monitoring and reporting on heating/cooling decarbonisation
content: Annual reporting on progress on RES indicators, emissions and prices

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Setting a national indicative share of energy from renewable sources in buildings
content: Setting a national indicative share of RES in the final energy consumption of the building sector by 2030
amending_legislation: Update of the INECP; decision of the Council of Ministers

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Integration of RES requirements into the energy performance of buildings
content: Introduction of quantitative requirements for a minimum share of RES in new construction and deep renovation, in line with the EPBD (zero-rated buildings)

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Solar readiness for new buildings
content: Obligation for all new buildings to be designed with design and technical readiness for RES (PV and solar thermal systems)
amending_legislation: OSH; EEOSS; Regulation No RD-02-20-3 of 9.11.2022

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Regulatory framework for RES in multi-apartment buildings
content: Facilitating the collective installation and sharing of RES in co-ownership

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Development of energy communities in the building sector
content: Optimising the conditions for shared production and consumption in buildings and neighbourhoods, including network charges and access to data
amending_legislation: Energy Act; Regulation No 6 of 28.3.2024; Rules for measuring electricity; Regulation No E-RD-04-1 of 12.3.2020

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Financial incentives for RES in buildings
content: Combined grant and loan instruments for households, municipalities and vulnerable groups

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: RES clearance centres (OSS)
content: Building an OSS network of technical and administrative support for citizens and municipalities

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: National register of RES in buildings
content: Establishment of a public register of RES installations linked to energy performance certificates

section_topic: Promoting the use of RES in buildings
measure_name: Monitoring and annual reporting on RES in buildings
content: Annual progress report on the share of RES in buildings and the contribution to the INECP objectives

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in new public and non-residential buildings above 250 m2
content: Mandatory design and construction of a photovoltaic or solar thermal installation, where technically and economically feasible
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, EE and RES Programme, Recovery and Resilience Facility

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in existing public buildings above 2000 m2
content: Installation of solar devices during major renovation or reconstruction
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, European Public Buildings Programmes

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in existing public buildings above 750 m2
content: Mandatory inclusion of solar systems in case of overhaul or renovation of the roof
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, Regions in Growth Programme

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in all public buildings above 250 m2
content: Completion of solar energy in public buildings and integration into building energy management systems
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, EE and RES, future public sector programmes

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in existing non-residential buildings above 500 m2
content: Installation of solar devices when renovating a roof or changing building systems
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, private investments, energy service contracts

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in new residential buildings
content: Mandatory design and construction of solar installations (photovoltaics or solar thermal)
sources_of_funding: National Decarbonisation Fund, Mortgages and Green Credits, Private Investment

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Solar installations in new covered car parks
content: Installation of photovoltaic sheds or panels to power common needs and charging stations
sources_of_funding: Private investment, NFC, sustainable mobility programmes

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Development of national criteria and exemptions under Article 10
content: Clear rules for the implementation and justified release of protected sites

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Simplified notification procedure and digital connection platform
content: Reducing the administrative time for accession to less than 1 month

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: National programme for solar installations in public buildings
content: Achieved coverage of more than 80% of the public building stock

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Integrated solutions (PV + storage + management)
content: Increasing self-consumption and network flexibility

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: Training and qualification of designers and installers
content: Creation of a network of certified specialists for RES installations

section_topic: Deployment of solar energy in buildings
measure_name: National Solar Installation Register
content: Full traceability and visibility of progress

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Regulatory requirements
measure_name: Regulation on the calculation of life cycle carbon emissions (LCC) of buildings
content: Mandatory emission estimation for modules A1 to A5, B4 to B5, C1 to C4 and benefit/load accounting D; methodology harmonised with Level(s) and EN 15978/EN 15804
time_limit: Q1-Q4 2026; entry into force 2027; biennial review

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Regulatory requirements
measure_name: Setting first carbon footprint limits for new buildings (Article 7(5) EPBD)
content: Differentiation by building type; a stepwise tightening mechanism every 2 years; introduction of mandatory life cycle analysis for major renovations

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Regulatory requirements
measure_name: Amendments to the Spatial Planning Act (ZUT)
content: Registration of the LCA assessment as part of the investment project and as a condition for building permit; requirement for a selective demolition audit

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Regulatory requirements
measure_name: Amendments to the Energy Performance of Buildings Regulations
content: Introducing a carbon storage and total life cycle emissions indicator in the energy performance certificate; introduction of a digital material passport

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Regulatory requirements
measure_name: Methodological guidance for the application of Level(s)
content: Calculation templates, reference values for verification/confirmation; EPD requirements and database maintenance

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Circular economy
measure_name: Update of the Construction Waste Ordinance
content: Mandatory pre-audits and selective demolition plans; preparing for re-use/recycling targets >=70% total weight; digital declaration for material recovery

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Circular economy
measure_name: Standards and guidelines for reuse and recycled materials
content: Technical specifications for the reuse of components; quality control of recycled aggregates

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Circular economy
measure_name: Regional Hubs/Material Depots
content: Network of regional centres for CDW processing and reuse; certified shredding and sorting stations

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Low carbon materials and carbon storage
measure_name: Criteria in public procurement
content: Requirements for a minimum share of recycled and natural materials; inclusion of carbon saving indicators in public procurement evaluation

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Low carbon materials and carbon storage
measure_name: Carbon Storage in Buildings Pilot Programme
content: Grant/bonus mechanisms for projects with proven carbon storage capacity (e.g. using natural materials such as wood, hemp, straw); preparation for CRCF certification

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Low carbon materials and carbon storage
measure_name: Guide to Environmental Solutions
content: Technical details and fire safety, acoustic solutions, durability and maintenance of materials; examples of circular economy design systems

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Data traceability and control
measure_name: National registry of total carbon footprint of buildings
content: Integration with the register of energy certificates and the digital building register; design and as-built values; indicators for public projects

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Data traceability and control
measure_name: Verification system
content: Requirements for independent verification of life cycle calculations; need for accreditation of verifiers

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Data traceability and control
measure_name: Annual report on total life cycle greenhouse gas emissions from buildings
content: Average values per building type, application of limit values, reuse/recycling effect and carbon storage

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Programmes and funding
measure_name: NDF Low-carbon refurbishment
content: Additional bonus with demonstrated life-cycle carbon savings and/or stored carbon

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Programmes and funding
measure_name: Circular economy models
content: Financing and incentivising selective demolition, conducting audits, sorting by fractions; mandatory control and monitoring requirements

section_topic: Life-cycle GHG emissions - Programmes and funding
measure_name: Regional material landfills
content: Infrastructure and start-up capacity (Operator + Lab)

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Inventory of heated/cooled public buildings over 250 m2
content: Preparation and publication of a complete inventory of heated and/or cooled buildings over 250 m2 owned or occupied by state, regional and municipal authorities
lead_institution: AUER, municipalities, regional governors, line ministries
sources_of_funding: National register of public buildings; link to EPC Database

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Annual reporting on 3% refurbishment and 1.9% consumption reduction
content: Introduction of a mechanism for annual reporting on progress towards the 3% refurbishment and 1.9% final consumption reduction requirement
sources_of_funding: Reports on municipal EE plans; National analysis of EE policies

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Alternative approach with renovation passports for staged renovation
content: Applying an alternative approach with renovation passports for staged renovation to reach NZEB or NZEB level
sources_of_funding: Integration with National Renovation Plan; LIFE CET and ELENA for project preparation

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Renovation of 3% of total floor area of public buildings annually
content: Renovation of at least 3% of the total floor area of heated and/or cooled public buildings larger than 250 m2
lead_institution: Building owners – state and municipal authorities
sources_of_funding: National EE programmes; Modernisation Fund; Cohesion Policy 2021-2027

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Energy efficiency first principle in procurement
content: Application of the energy efficiency first principle in the procurement of buildings products and services
sources_of_funding: Green public procurement; methodologies for life-cycle costing

section_topic: Improvement of buildings owned by public bodies
measure_name: Public reporting on renovation results and energy savings
content: Public reporting on the results of renovation and energy savings in the public sector
sources_of_funding: National annual energy efficiency report; EU Building Stock Observatory

section_topic: Bridging skills gap - education and training
measure_name: Strengthening the role of Sector Skills Councils and Sector Organisations
content: Institutionalise the leading role of Sector Skills Councils and sectoral organisations (CCB, CAB, KIIP, Chamber of Installers, etc.) in defining competency profiles, formulating continuing qualification requirements
amending_legislation: Amending the Vocational Education and Training Act (VET)
lead_institution: Mont, NAVET, sector skills councils, industry and professional organisations

section_topic: Bridging skills gap - education and training
measure_name: Updating curricula
content: Update vocational training curricula and examination programmes for Architecture and Construction and Electrical and Energy professions, including energy efficiency, RES, NZEB, smart systems, performance and circular economy
amending_legislation: Amendments to State Education Standards and Regulations to the VET Act
lead_institution: Mont, NVET, vocational high schools, sector skills councils

section_topic: Bridging skills gap - education and training
measure_name: National Continuing Vocational Training (CVT) system for all levels
content: Establish a single national framework for continuing vocational training covering workers, technicians, planners, architects and engineers; introduction of modular trainings, micro-credentials and CVT credits
amending_legislation: Amending VET; amendment of the Higher Education Act (HEA)
lead_institution: Mont, NVET, higher education institutions, sectoral organisations, professional chambers
sources_of_funding: ESF+; Education Programme; National Decarbonisation Fund; private co-financing

section_topic: Bridging skills gap - education and training
measure_name: Training and qualification of teachers and trainers
content: Systematic upskilling of teachers in vocational secondary schools, vocational training centres and higher education institutions on EE, RES, smart technologies and performance
amending_legislation: Amendments to Regulations to the VET Act and HEA on qualification and status of teachers
lead_institution: Mont, NAVET, higher education institutions, industry organisations

section_topic: Bridging skills gap - education and training
measure_name: National system for monitoring and forecasting skills needs
content: Development of an integrated system for annual monitoring and forecasting of workforce needs by qualification level and region, using data from the construction sector, energy programmes and regional plans
amending_legislation: Secondary amendments to the VET Act introducing a mandatory cycle of analysis
lead_institution: Mont, NAVET, NSI, MLSP, FAC, Sector Skills Councils

section_topic: Awareness-raising campaigns and advisory tools
measure_name: Mandatory minimum budget for communication in renovation programmes
content: Introduce a requirement for all public building renovation programmes to allocate a minimum of 2% of the budget to targeted communication, information and consultancy activities integrated with the CCA
lead_institution: MA, MRDPW, MF, programme managing authorities
sources_of_funding: The budgets of the programmes concerned

section_topic: Awareness-raising campaigns and advisory tools
measure_name: Mandatory budget for monitoring evaluation and reporting
content: Introduction of a minimum of 2% of the budget for technical follow-up, measurement and verification of results, behavioural analysis and public reporting
lead_institution: ME, AUER, MRDPW, MoF, Managing Authorities
sources_of_funding: The budgets of the programmes concerned

section_topic: Awareness-raising campaigns and advisory tools
measure_name: National communication and monitoring methodology
content: Development of a standardised methodology and KPI set for communication campaigns and for monitoring the impact of programmes used by all managing authorities
amending_legislation: Methodological act/regulation of the Energy Efficiency Act or decision of the Council of Ministers
lead_institution: ME, AUER, MS, NSORB, universities and NGOs

section_topic: Awareness-raising campaigns and advisory tools
measure_name: Integration of monitoring with digital registries and CCA
content: Link communication, training and monitoring activities with national digital registries (certificates, building renovation passports) and CCA activities
